edges.evidence: This personalized federated learning paper directly builds upon the foundational FL framework and challenges outlined in the 2020 advances survey.
Applies federated learning principles to vehicular IoT domain, extending the general FL framework to specific automotive use cases.
Combines federated learning with blockchain for health IoT, extending FL principles to address healthcare-specific trust and provenance requirements.
Directly addresses privacy concerns in federated learning by proposing homomorphic encryption solutions to FL's identified challenges.
Implements privacy-preserving FL using TEEs, directly tackling the privacy leakage problems highlighted in the FL advances survey.
Optimizes federated learning for edge computing environments, addressing computational efficiency challenges identified in the foundational FL work.
Applies self-supervised federated learning to medical imaging, extending FL principles to address data heterogeneity in healthcare domains.
Both papers investigate the same phenomenon of model collapse from training on generated data, with very similar titles and content.
The hallucination survey builds upon BIG-bench's systematic evaluation framework to understand LLM failure modes and capabilities.
This evaluation survey directly builds upon BIG-bench's comprehensive benchmarking approach for assessing LLM capabilities.
Applies systematic evaluation principles from BIG-bench to legal domain assessment, extending benchmarking to professional competency testing.
Uses insights from the hallucination survey to develop fake news detection methods that address LLM reliability issues.
Applies evaluation methodologies from the LLM survey to psychological text analysis, extending evaluation frameworks to new domains.
Applies systematic LLM evaluation principles to mental health domain, extending benchmarking methodologies to healthcare applications.
Uses evaluation frameworks from the LLM survey to assess ChatGPT's affective computing capabilities in specialized domains.

notes: Identified 15 new edges, primarily federated learning extensions/applications and LLM evaluation methodology chains. Several domain-specific applications extend foundational frameworks.
